title: Pricing table is hardcoded string-match cascade duplicating model knowledge
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
dry
## Location
src/session/pricing.rs:18-131
## Violation
get_pricing is a 110-line if/else cascade matching model substrings — annotated allow(clippy::too_many_lines). Adds a new model = edit pricing.rs. The same model knowledge exists in src/proxy.rs (determine_provider) and src/providers/* (chat_endpoint, get_headers). A model rename or repricing requires coordinated edits across at least 3 files. The cascade order is also order-sensitive — m.contains("gpt-5") matches before m.contains("gpt-4"), and m.contains("gemini-2") must come before m.contains("gemini") — fragile undocumented ordering.

Cache-write factor 1.25× and cache-read factor 0.1× are hard-coded in calculate_cost without per-model overrides; in reality each provider has different cache pricing.
## Mandated refactor
Externalize pricing into a static toml/json table loaded once at startup. Structure: { 'claude-3-opus': { input: 15.0, output: 75.0, cache_read_factor: 0.1, cache_write_factor: 1.25 } } with explicit model patterns (regex or glob), not substring matching. Add a fallback hook the operator can configure.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
